mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS relative to recent months lowers discount rates and raises equity valuation multiples, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is falling.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not above baseline after FEDFUNDS declines, or if triggered periods show negative average returns in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a stress threshold signals worsening labor demand and weaker household income expectations, which compresses near-term equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS indicates improving final demand and revenue growth for firms, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 1-month changes in RSAFS do not precede above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal loses sign consistency across consecutive rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A strong monthly rise in INDPRO relative to recent history signals accelerating industrial output and cyclical growth, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases month-over-month by at least 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO growth triggers fail to generate positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the short-horizon effect disappears in a 3-year rolling test.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls while UNRATE rises, the joint signal identifies broad demand contraction in housing and labor markets, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-down and UNRATE-up pattern does not outperform each series alone in predicting negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, an increase in FEDFUNDS relative to CPIAUCSL inflation implies real-rate pressure and multiple compression, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if tightening episodes with rising FEDFUNDS and sticky CPIAUCSL fail to produce below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the spread-like joint effect is not negative in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: If PAYEMS is rising while UNRATE is falling, labor-market improvement signals stronger income growth and lower recession risk, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months and PAYEMS rises over the same span
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down trigger does not improve 60-day SPX return hit rate versus either proxy alone, or if triggered returns are not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive and RSAFS growth is also positive, the economy is broadening from output recovery into final-demand recovery, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDP and retail-sales growth does not lead to above-unconditional 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal is not stronger than single-series effects in walk-forward tests.
